chunks.text:
# Manufacturing Business Glossary

This glossary defines the core business concepts and terminology used in the manufacturing supply chain domain. It covers product structures, materials management, production workflows, and quality assurance processes.

## Product

A Product represents any manufactured item in the system, including finished goods ready for sale, sub-assemblies used in production, and individual components. Products are organized in a hierarchical structure where complex product... [TRUNCATED]

## Warehouse

A Warehouse represents a physical storage location where materials, components, and finished goods are stored. Warehouses have defined capacity limits, geographic location information, and are managed by designated managers. Warehouses serve as nodes in the supply chain network where inventory is held, shipments are received, and products are staged for production or distribution.

**Key Attributes:**
- Unique warehouse identifier
- Warehouse name and location
- Address, city, and ... [TRUNCATED]

## QualityControl

Quality Control (QC) represents inspection and testing activities performed on materials, in-process production, or finished goods. QC records track the type of inspection (incoming, in-process, final), inspection date, inspector, test results (pass, fail, conditional), defect counts, and notes. QC activities are associated with specific production batches and may reference technical specifications being tested.

**Key Attributes:**
- Unique QC identifier
- Batch reference (pr... [TRUNCATED]

# Manufacturing Data Dictionary

This data dictionary documents the database schema for the manufacturing supply chain system. It includes all tables, columns, data types, constraints, and relationships. Special attention is given to self-referential relationships that support hierarchical product structures.

## product

Stores all manufactured items including finished goods, assemblies, and components. Supports hierarchical product relationships via self-referencing foreign key.

| Column | Da... [TRUNCATED]

## bom

Bill of Materials defining product composition and hierarchical structure. Supports recursive relationships for multi-level assemblies.

| Column | Data Type | Constraints | Description |
|--------|-----------|-------------|-------------|
| bom_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique BOM identifier |
| parent_product_id | VARCHAR(20) | NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY → product(product_id) | Parent product (what is being built) |
| component_product_id | VARCHAR(20) | NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY → produc... [TRUNCATED]

## warehouse

Stores physical storage location information for inventory management.

| Column | Data Type | Constraints | Description |
|--------|-----------|-------------|-------------|
| warehouse_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique warehouse identifier |
| warehouse_name | VARCHAR(100) | NOT NULL | Warehouse name |
| address | VARCHAR(200) | | Street address |
| city | VARCHAR(50) | | City location |
| state | VARCHAR(50) | | State or province |
| capacity | INT | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0, CH... [TRUNCATED]

## work_order

Represents production jobs for manufacturing products with hierarchical work order support.

| Column | Data Type | Constraints | Description |
|--------|-----------|-------------|-------------|
| work_order_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique work order identifier |
| product_id | VARCHAR(20) | NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY → product(product_id) | Product to manufacture |
| parent_work_order | VARCHAR(20) | FOREIGN KEY → work_order(work_order_id) | Parent work order for decomposition... [TRUNCATED]

## specification

Defines technical requirements and acceptance criteria for materials and products.

| Column | Data Type | Constraints | Description |
|--------|-----------|-------------|-------------|
| specification_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique specification identifier |
| specification_name | VARCHAR(100) | NOT NULL | Specification name |
| version | VARCHAR(10) | NOT NULL | Specification version |
| effective_date | DATE | NOT NULL | Effective date |
| spec_type | spec_type_t | ... [TRUNCATED]

## shipment

Tracks material transfers between warehouses, suppliers, and customers.

| Column | Data Type | Constraints | Description |
|--------|-----------|-------------|-------------|
| shipment_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique shipment identifier |
| shipment_type | shipment_type_t | NOT NULL | INBOUND, OUTBOUND, INTERNAL |
| warehouse_id | VARCHAR(20) | FOREIGN KEY → warehouse(warehouse_id) | Warehouse reference |
| supplier_id | VARCHAR(20) | FOREIGN KEY → supplier(supplier_id) | S... [TRUNCATED]

triplets.subject: Each product
triplets.object: a unique identifier, name, type classification, base cost, and lead time

triplets.subject: Component
triplets.object: atomic raw materials or purchased parts

triplets.subject: BOM
triplets.object: a parent product, a component product, the required quantity, unit of measure, and hierarchical leve... [TRUNCATED]

triplets.subject: Supplier
triplets.object: raw materials, components, or finished goods

triplets.subject: Warehouses
triplets.object: nodes in the supply chain network

entities_post_resolution.name: Component
entities_post_resolution.definition: A Component is an atomic raw material or purchased part used in manufacturing, which may be referenced in specifications, inventory, or as part of a product.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: A Component represents atomic raw materials or purchased parts that cannot be further broken down into simpler manufactured items. | Components may reference Specifications for quality requirements | | component_id | VARCHAR(20) | NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY → component(component_id) | Component reference | | | component_product_id | VARCHAR(20) | NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY → product(product_id) | Component product (what goes into it) | | Components are supplied by multiple Suppliers (many-to-many) | Components have Inventory records tracking stock levels | Stores atomic raw materials and purchased parts that are procured from external suppliers. | | component_id | VARCHAR(20) | FOREIGN KEY → component(component_id) | Component reference (exclusive with product_id) |

entities_post_resolution.name: Contact Email Address
entities_post_resolution.definition: A contact email address is an electronic mail address used to communicate with an individual or organization.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | contact_email | VARCHAR(100) | | Contact email address |

entities_post_resolution.name: Inspection Date
entities_post_resolution.definition: The specific date when an inspection is conducted, recorded for quality control and tracking purposes.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | qc_date | DATE | NOT NULL | Inspection date | | QC records track the type of inspection (incoming, in-process, final), inspection date, inspector, test results (pass, fail, conditional), defect counts, and notes.

entities_post_resolution.name: Inventory
entities_post_resolution.definition: Inventory represents the stock of components and products stored in warehouses, including records that track quantities, locations, and identifiers.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: Inventory represents the actual stock levels of components and products stored in specific warehouses. | Each inventory record tracks the quantity on hand, quantity reserved for pending orders, and reorder thresholds to trigger procurement. | Inventory records support either components or products (but not both in the same record) and include timestamps for the last restock date. | Products are stored in Inventory records | Components have Inventory records tracking stock levels | Tracks stock levels for components and products at specific warehouse locations. | | inventory_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique inventory identifier |

entities_post_resolution.name: Parent Product
entities_post_resolution.definition: A parent product is a product that is referenced by other products in a hierarchical assembly or relationship, serving as a component or assembly in a product structure.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | parent_product_id | VARCHAR(20) | NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY → product(product_id) | Parent product (what is being built) | | A Product can reference a parent Product (for multi-level assemblies) | parent_product_id | VARCHAR(20) | FOREIGN KEY → product(product_id) | Parent product for hierarchical assemblies | product_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique product identifier | | product_id | VARCHAR(20) | FOREIGN KEY → product(product_id) | Product reference (exclusive with component_id) | | | product_id | VARCHAR(20) | NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY → product(product_id) | Product to manufacture |

entities_post_resolution.name: Product
entities_post_resolution.definition: A Product represents any manufactured item in the system, including finished goods, sub-assemblies, components, and their classifications.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: A Product represents any manufactured item in the system, including finished goods ready for sale, sub-assemblies used in production, and individual components. | Batches produce a specific Product | Products are organized in a hierarchical structure where complex products can contain simpler products as parts. | Products are used in Work Orders for production | Products appear in Shipments (outbound) | Products are stored in Inventory records | Stores all manufactured items including finished goods, assemblies, and components. | Supports hierarchical product relationships via self-referencing foreign key. | | product_id | VARCHAR(20) | FOREIGN KEY → product(product_id) | Product reference (exclusive with component_id) | | product_name | VARCHAR(100) | NOT NULL | Product name | product_type | product_type_t | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 'FINISHED_GOOD' | Product classification: FINISHED_GOOD, ASSEMBLY, COMPONENT

entities_post_resolution.name: Quantity Completed
entities_post_resolution.definition: The quantity of items or tasks that have been finished or fulfilled in a business process.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | quantity_completed | DECIMAL(10,2) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0.00, CHECK >= 0, <= ordered | Quantity completed |

entities_post_resolution.name: Quantity To Produce
entities_post_resolution.definition: Quantity to produce refers to the planned amount of product scheduled for manufacturing, whereas quantity produced indicates the actual amount of product manufactured.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | quantity_ordered | DECIMAL(10,2) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0.00, CHECK > 0 | Quantity to produce | | Batches record the product produced, production date, quantity produced, warehouse location, expiry date (if applicable), and quality control status.

entities_post_resolution.name: Shipment
entities_post_resolution.definition: A Shipment is the physical transfer of materials between locations such as warehouses, suppliers, and customers within a supply chain.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: A Shipment represents the physical transfer of materials between warehouses, from suppliers to warehouses (inbound), or from warehouses to customers (outbound). | Products appear in Shipments (outbound) | Shipments connect Warehouses, Suppliers, and Customers. | Shipments update Inventory levels.

entities_post_resolution.name: Technical Specification
entities_post_resolution.definition: A technical specification is a detailed document that defines the technical requirements, standards, and criteria for materials, products, or processes.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: A Specification defines technical requirements, standards, and acceptance criteria for materials or products. | Specifications include version numbers, effective dates, specification types (dimensional, material, performance), critical parameters to test, and acceptable value ranges with units of measure. | Components may reference Specifications for quality requirements | specification_id | VARCHAR(20) | | Technical specification reference | QC activities are associated with specific production batches and may reference technical specifications being tested.

entities_post_resolution.name: Supplier
entities_post_resolution.definition: A supplier is an external vendor or company that provides raw materials, components, or finished goods to an organization.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: A Supplier is an external vendor or company that provides raw materials, components, or finished goods to the manufacturing organization. | | supplier_id | VARCHAR(20) | NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY → supplier(supplier_id) | Supplier reference | | Suppliers are associated with components they can provide through approved supplier relationships.

entities_post_resolution.name: Supplier
entities_post_resolution.definition: An entity that supplies goods or services to an organization, identified by unique identifiers and associated with specific names.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: A Supplier is an external vendor or company that provides raw materials, components, or finished goods to the manufacturing organization. | | supplier_id | VARCHAR(20) | NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY → supplier(supplier_id) | Supplier reference | | Suppliers are associated with components they can provide through approved supplier relationships. | | supplier_name | VARCHAR(100) | NOT NULL | Supplier name | | Stores external vendor information for component procurement. | | supplier_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique supplier identifier |

entities_post_resolution.name: Warehouse
entities_post_resolution.definition: A warehouse is a physical storage location used for storing materials, components, and finished goods within a supply chain network.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: A Warehouse represents a physical storage location where materials, components, and finished goods are stored. | Warehouses have defined capacity limits, geographic location information, and are managed by designated managers. | Warehouses serve as nodes in the supply chain network where inventory is held, shipments are received, and products are staged for production or distribution. | Stores physical storage location information for inventory management. | | warehouse_id | VARCHAR(20) | NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY → warehouse(warehouse_id) | Warehouse location | | Batches record the product produced, production date, quantity produced, warehouse location, expiry date (if applicable), and quality control status. | | warehouse_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique warehouse identifier | | | warehouse_id | VARCHAR(20) | FOREIGN KEY → warehouse(warehouse_id) | Production location | | | warehouse_name | VARCHAR(100) | NOT NULL | Warehouse name |

entities_post_resolution.name: Work Order
entities_post_resolution.definition: A work order is a formal document or record that authorizes and details a specific production job or manufacturing task to produce a designated quantity of a product.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: A Work Order represents a production job or manufacturing run to produce a specified quantity of a product. | Products are used in Work Orders for production | Work Orders track planned versus completed quantities, status through the production lifecycle, priority levels, and planned dates. | Work Orders can have hierarchical relationships where a parent work order may decompose into child work orders for different production stages or sub-assemblies. | Represents production jobs for manufacturing products with hierarchical work order support. | | work_order_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique work order identifier |

entities_post_resolution.name: Production Job
entities_post_resolution.definition: A production job is a specific manufacturing task or process assigned to produce a certain quantity of a product within a manufacturing system.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: A Work Order represents a production job or manufacturing run to produce a specified quantity of a product. | Represents production jobs for manufacturing products with hierarchical work order support.

entities_post_resolution.name: Manufactured Item
entities_post_resolution.definition: A manufactured item is any product or component produced through a manufacturing process, including finished goods, sub-assemblies, and individual parts.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: A Product represents any manufactured item in the system, including finished goods ready for sale, sub-assemblies used in production, and individual components. | Stores all manufactured items including finished goods, assemblies, and components.

entities_post_resolution.name: Component Supplier Id
entities_post_resolution.definition: A component_id is a unique identifier for a component within a system.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | component_supplier_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique relationship identifier | | component_id | VARCHAR(20) | PRIMARY KEY | Unique component identifier | | component_id | VARCHAR(20) | NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY → component(component_id) | Component reference | | | component_id | VARCHAR(20) | FOREIGN KEY → component(component_id) | Component reference (exclusive with product_id) | | | component_product_id | VARCHAR(20) | NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY → product(product_id) | Component product (what goes into it) | | Junction table linking components to their approved suppliers with pricing and lead time information.

entities_post_resolution.name: Expiry Date
entities_post_resolution.definition: The expiry date is the specific date after which a product, especially perishable goods, is no longer safe or suitable for sale or consumption.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: Batches record the product produced, production date, quantity produced, warehouse location, expiry date (if applicable), and quality control status. | Batches may have expiry dates for perishable goods

entities_post_resolution.name: Hierarchical Product Relationships
entities_post_resolution.definition: Hierarchical product relationships refer to the structured associations where products or work orders are organized in parent-child hierarchies to represent dependencies or decomposition.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: Supports hierarchical product relationships via self-referencing foreign key. | Work Orders can have hierarchical relationships where a parent work order may decompose into child work orders for different production stages or sub-assemblies.

entities_post_resolution.name: Last Restock Timestamp
entities_post_resolution.definition: The last restock timestamp is the date and time when the inventory was most recently replenished.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | last_restock_date | DATE | | Last restock timestamp |

entities_post_resolution.name: End Dates
entities_post_resolution.definition: These entities represent the scheduled start and end dates for a planned activity or project phase.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | planned_end_date | DATE | | Planned end date | | | planned_start_date | DATE | | Planned start date |

entities_post_resolution.name: Priority
entities_post_resolution.definition: An attribute indicating the urgency or importance level of a task or issue, typically categorized as LOW, MEDIUM, HIGH, or URGENT.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | priority | priority_t | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 'MEDIUM' | LOW, MEDIUM, HIGH, URGENT |

entities_post_resolution.name: Qc Type
entities_post_resolution.definition: qc_type is a classification representing the type of quality control process applied during different stages such as incoming, in process, or final.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | qc_type | qc_type_t | NOT NULL | INCOMING, IN_PROCESS, FINAL |

entities_post_resolution.name: Quantity On Hand
entities_post_resolution.definition: The quantity on hand is the current available amount of inventory in stock, used for tracking and managing inventory levels.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | quantity_on_hand | DECIMAL(10,2) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0.00, CHECK >= 0 | Available quantity | | Each inventory record tracks the quantity on hand, quantity reserved for pending orders, and reorder thresholds to trigger procurement.

entities_post_resolution.name: Quantity Reserved
entities_post_resolution.definition: The reserved quantity represents the amount of stock or resources that has been set aside and is not available for general use.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | quantity_reserved | DECIMAL(10,2) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0.00, CHECK >= 0 | Reserved quantity |

entities_post_resolution.name: Status
entities_post_resolution.definition: An attribute indicating the current state or phase of a process or item, such as 'PENDING', 'IN_PROGRESS', 'COMPLETED', or 'CLOSED'.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | status | status_t | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 'PENDING' | PENDING, IN_PROGRESS, COMPLETED, CLOSED |

entities_post_resolution.name: Negotiated Unit Price
entities_post_resolution.definition: An entity representing the price agreed upon for a single unit of a product or service in a negotiation context.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | unit_price | DECIMAL(10,2) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0.00, CHECK >= 0 | Negotiated unit price | | | unit_of_measure | VARCHAR(10) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 'EA' | Unit of measure | | base_cost | DECIMAL(10,2) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0.00, CHECK >= 0 | Base manufacturing cost | standard_cost | DECIMAL(10,2) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0.00, CHECK >= 0 | Standard procurement cost | unit_of_measure | VARCHAR(10) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 'EA' | Unit of measure (EA, KG, M, L, etc.)

entities_post_resolution.name: Number Of Defects Found
entities_post_resolution.definition: The total number of defects identified in a product or process during quality control inspections.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | defect_count | INT | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0, CHECK >= 0 | Number of defects found | | QC records track the type of inspection (incoming, in-process, final), inspection date, inspector, test results (pass, fail, conditional), defect counts, and notes.

entities_post_resolution.name: Performance Rating
entities_post_resolution.definition: A performance rating is a numerical score, typically between 1.0 and 5.0, used to evaluate an individual's or entity's performance.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | rating | DECIMAL(3,2) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 3.00, CHECK 1.0-5.0 | Performance rating |

entities_post_resolution.name: Procurement Lead Time
entities_post_resolution.definition: Procurement lead time is the duration required to acquire goods or services from the initiation of the purchase process to the receipt of the items.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | lead_time_days | INT | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0 | Procurement lead time | | lead_time_days | INT | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0, CHECK >= 0 | Procurement or production lead time

entities_post_resolution.name: Cycle Time
entities_post_resolution.definition: Cycle time is the total time required to complete a process or operation from start to finish, including setup and processing durations.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: Each route step specifies the operation name, work center where the operation is performed, cycle time for the operation, setup time required, and sequence number.

entities_post_resolution.name: Default 0.00
entities_post_resolution.definition: These entities represent different types of data attributes: a status indicator, a lead time measurement, and cost values, each with their own specific meaning.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: base_cost | DECIMAL(10,2) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0.00, CHECK >= 0 | Base manufacturing cost | standard_cost | DECIMAL(10,2) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0.00, CHECK >= 0 | Standard procurement cost | is_active | yes_no_t | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 'Y' | Active status flag | lead_time_days | INT | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0, CHECK >= 0 | Procurement or production lead time

entities_post_resolution.name: Reorder Trigger Level
entities_post_resolution.definition: The reorder trigger level is a predefined inventory quantity that, when reached, prompts the system to initiate a reorder to replenish stock.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | reorder_threshold | DECIMAL(10,2) | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0.00 | Reorder trigger level |

entities_post_resolution.name: Product
entities_post_resolution.definition: A Product can have multiple child Products (via BOM hierarchy) | A Product can reference a parent Product (for multi-level assemblies)
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: A Product can have multiple child Products (via BOM hierarchy) | A Product can reference a parent Product (for multi-level assemblies)

entities_post_resolution.name: Bom
entities_post_resolution.definition: The Bill of Materials (BOM) defines the composition of products by specifying which components and sub-assemblies are required to manufacture a parent product. | Each BOM entry indicates a parent product, a component product (which can be either a component or sub-assembly), the required quantity, unit of measure, and hierarchical level in the product structure. | BOM entries support recursive relationships to represent multi-level product assemblies.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: The Bill of Materials (BOM) defines the composition of products by specifying which components and sub-assemblies are required to manufacture a parent product. | Each BOM entry indicates a parent product, a component product (which can be either a component or sub-assembly), the required quantity, unit of measure, and hierarchical level in the product structure. | BOM entries support recursive relationships to represent multi-level product assemblies.

entities_post_resolution.name: Bom Levels
entities_post_resolution.definition: BOM levels support hierarchical product explosions
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: BOM levels support hierarchical product explosions

entities_post_resolution.name: Batch
entities_post_resolution.definition: Batches produce a specific Product | Batches record the product produced, production date, quantity produced, warehouse location, expiry date (if applicable), and quality control status. | Batches record the product produced, production date, quantity produced, warehouse location, expiry date (if applicable), and quality control status.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: Batches produce a specific Product | Batches record the product produced, production date, quantity produced, warehouse location, expiry date (if applicable), and quality control status. | Batches record the product produced, production date, quantity produced, warehouse location, expiry date (if applicable), and quality control status.

entities_post_resolution.definition: Bill of Materials defining product composition and hierarchical structure.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: Bill of Materials defining product composition and hierarchical structure.

entities_post_resolution.definition: | bom_level | INT | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0, CHECK >= 0 | Depth in product hierarchy |
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: | bom_level | INT | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 0, CHECK >= 0 | Depth in product hierarchy |

entities_post_resolution.name: Product
entities_post_resolution.definition: Each product has a unique identifier, name, type classification, base cost, and lead time for procurement or production.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: Each product has a unique identifier, name, type classification, base cost, and lead time for procurement or production.

entities_post_resolution.definition: | inspector_id | VARCHAR(20) | | Inspector reference |

entities_post_resolution.definition: Shipments update Inventory levels.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: Shipments update Inventory levels.

entities_post_resolution.definition: | parent_work_order | VARCHAR(20) | FOREIGN KEY → work_order(work_order_id) | Parent work order for decomposition |

entities_post_resolution.definition: | is_preferred | yes_no_t | NOT NULL, DEFAULT 'N' | Preferred supplier flag |

entities_post_resolution.definition: QC records track the type of inspection (incoming, in-process, final), inspection date, inspector, test results (pass, fail, conditional), defect counts, and notes. | QC records track the type of inspection (incoming, in-process, final), inspection date, inspector, test results (pass, fail, conditional), defect counts, and notes. | QC records track the type of inspection (incoming, in-process, final), inspection date, inspector, test results (pass, fail, conditional), defect counts, and notes.

entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: Batches record the product produced, production date, quantity produced, warehouse location, expiry date (if applicable), and quality control status.

entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: Batches undergo Quality Control inspections

entities_post_resolution.name: Route
entities_post_resolution.definition: A Route defines the sequence of manufacturing operations or steps required to produce a product. | Each route step specifies the operation name, work center where the operation is performed, cycle time for the operation, setup time required, and sequence number. | Each route step specifies the operation name, work center where the operation is performed, cycle time for the operation, setup time required, and sequence number.

entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: Multiple BOM entries define a complete product structure

entities_post_resolution.name: Hierarchical Structure
entities_post_resolution.definition: Products are organized in a hierarchical structure where complex products can contain simpler products as parts.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: Products are organized in a hierarchical structure where complex products can contain simpler products as parts.

entities_post_resolution.name: Physical Storage Location
entities_post_resolution.definition: A Warehouse represents a physical storage location where materials, components, and finished goods are stored.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: A Warehouse represents a physical storage location where materials, components, and finished goods are stored.

entities_post_resolution.name: Acceptable Value Range
entities_post_resolution.definition: Specifications include version numbers, effective dates, specification types (dimensional, material, performance), critical parameters to test, and acceptable value ranges with units of measure.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: Specifications include version numbers, effective dates, specification types (dimensional, material, performance), critical parameters to test, and acceptable value ranges with units of measure.

entities_post_resolution.name: Atomic Raw Materials
entities_post_resolution.definition: A Component represents atomic raw materials or purchased parts that cannot be further broken down into simpler manufactured items.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: A Component represents atomic raw materials or purchased parts that cannot be further broken down into simpler manufactured items.

entities_post_resolution.name: Either Components Or Products

tables_enriched.name: Product
tables_enriched.description: This table stores information about products, including their identifiers, names, types, hierarchical relationships, costs, lead times, and active status.

tables_enriched.name: Component
tables_enriched.description: This table stores information about various components used in manufacturing or assembly processes, including their identifiers, names, categories, measurement units, costs, and related specifications... [TRUNCATED]

tables_enriched.name: Bill of Materials
tables_enriched.description: This table stores the components and structure of products as part of the Bill of Materials, detailing how individual parts are assembled into finished products.

tables_enriched.name: Supplier
tables_enriched.description: This table stores information about suppliers providing goods or services to the company, including contact details and preference status.

tables_enriched.name: Warehouse
tables_enriched.description: This table stores information about warehouse locations, including their identifiers, names, addresses, capacities, and managers responsible for each warehouse.

tables_enriched.name: Inventory
tables_enriched.description: This table tracks the current inventory levels, stock reservations, and restocking information for products stored in various warehouses.

tables_enriched.name: Work Order
tables_enriched.description: This table stores information about manufacturing or maintenance work orders, including their status, priorities, associated products, quantities, and scheduled dates.

tables_enriched.name: Shipment
tables_enriched.description: This table records details about shipments, including their types, associated warehouses, suppliers, customers, and key dates related to shipment progress.

tables_enriched.name: Quality Control
tables_enriched.description: This table stores records of quality control inspections, including details about inspection batches, specifications, inspectors, results, and any defects identified.

tables_enriched.name: Specification
tables_enriched.description: This table stores specifications details, including versioning, effective dates, and critical parameters for various products or components.

tables_enriched.name: Batch
tables_enriched.description: This table records information about production batches of products, including quantities, production dates, warehouse locations, and quality control status.

tables_enriched.name: Route
tables_enriched.description: This table stores information about manufacturing or process routes, including the sequence of operations, associated products, and timing details.

tables_enriched.name: Component Supplier
tables_enriched.description: This table stores information about the suppliers for each component, including whether they are preferred suppliers, lead times, and pricing details.
